theory.intro: Multiplying by $10$, $100$ or $1000$ shifts every digit to a larger place value. Multiplying by their multiples (like $30$ or $400$) combines this with a basic fact.
facts: $\times 10$ adds one zero, $\times 100$ adds two, $\times 1000$ adds three.
Multiply the non-zero digits first, then attach the zeros.
steps: Multiply the leading (non-zero) digits using a basic fact.
Count the total number of zeros in the factors.
Attach that many zeros to the product.
